summary: Replaced the inline PowerPC assembly implementation of `ftNess_OnLoad` with matching C in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c`. The function now copies Ness special attributes from `ftData->ext_attr` into `Fighter::x2D8_specialAttributes2`, assigns `x2D4_specialAttributes` to that copied block, and registers Ness-related item resources from `ftdata->x48_items` with `func_8026B3F8` using item IDs `0x42` through `0x49`, plus `0x4E`, `0x65`, and `0x66`. The PR also removed local peephole pragmas around nearby functions after the asm removal.
what_changed: Converted `ftNess_OnLoad(HSD_GObj* gobj)` from an `asm` block to C.
Introduced local typed variables for `Fighter*`, `ftData*`, `void** items`, and `ftNessAttributes*` pointers.
Copied the external Ness attributes with `*sA2 = *ext_attr`, corresponding to the original loop copying the ext_attr block into `x2D8_specialAttributes2`.
Replaced repeated `lwz`/`li`/`bl func_8026B3F8` assembly calls with explicit `func_8026B3F8(items[index], id)` calls.
Preserved the non-linear item registration order from the assembly: indices `0,1,2,8,3,4,5,6,7,9,10` map to IDs `0x42,0x43,0x44,0x4E,0x45,0x46,0x47,0x48,0x49,0x65,0x66`.
Removed `#pragma push`, `#pragma peephole on`, and `#pragma pop` that had surrounded following functions after the asm implementation was replaced.
smart_moves: Used a whole-struct assignment between `ftNessAttributes*` values to reproduce the original bulk copy loop compactly and matchably.
Kept the item registration calls fully explicit rather than introducing a loop or table, preserving call order and avoiding codegen drift.
Pulled `ftdata->x48_items` into a local `void** items`, mirroring the original use of a saved item table pointer and making the repeated item accesses clearer.

Recognized that `x2D8_specialAttributes2` is the destination copy and `ftData->ext_attr` is the source, then assigned `x2D4_specialAttributes` to the destination as in the original `stw r6, 0x2d4(r7)`.
Preserved the unusual registration order where `items[8]`/ID `0x4E` is registered before `items[3]`/ID `0x45`, which was required by the original assembly.
decomp_lessons: For fighter `OnLoad` functions, check whether the assembly first copies `ftData->ext_attr` into `x2D8_specialAttributes2` and then points `x2D4_specialAttributes` at that copied per-fighter data.
Bulk attribute-copy loops can sometimes be expressed as `*dst = *src` if the special-attribute struct size and type are known; this may match better than manual word-copy code.
Do not assume item registration arrays are processed in numeric order. Preserve the exact load/call order shown by the assembly, especially when one item index appears out of sequence.
A local pointer to `ftdata->x48_items` can make repeated item-resource registrations cleaner while still matching the original register usage.
After removing an asm block, nearby compiler pragmas that were only needed for matching surrounding asm-era code may become unnecessary and should be reconsidered.
naming_conventions: `ftNess_OnLoad` follows the fighter-character lifecycle naming pattern `ft<Character>_OnLoad`.
`ftNessAttributes` is used as the typed struct for Ness-specific special attributes.

The existing project field names in this slice include `gobj->user_data`, `Fighter::x10C_ftData`, `ftData::x48_items`, `ftData::ext_attr`, `Fighter::x2D8_specialAttributes2`, and `Fighter::x2D4_specialAttributes`.
The PR used short locals `sA2`, `sA`, and `ext_attr`; however, `sA` appears unused in the diff excerpt, so future work should prefer clearer names or avoid unused locals when matching permits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The original assembly used `li r0, 0x1b` and a `bdnz` loop copying paired words, followed by one extra word, implying a fixed-size struct copy that was represented in C as `*sA2 = *ext_attr`.

The original source pointer came from `ft->x10C_ftData->ext_attr` and the destination from `ft->x2D8_specialAttributes2`; after copying, the destination pointer was stored into `ft->x2D4_specialAttributes`.
Repeated calls to `func_8026B3F8` were matched as explicit calls with hardcoded item IDs rather than generated from a loop.
The call sequence maps `items[8]` to `0x4E` before registering `items[3]` through `items[7]`, preserving instruction order from the original code.
The conversion eliminated the need for `nofralloc` inline assembly and the local peephole pragmas in this file section.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter `OnLoad` functions that copy `ftData->ext_attr` into `x2D8_specialAttributes2` and assign `x2D4_specialAttributes`.
Search for other character item registration sequences using `func_8026B3F8` to compare item index and ID ordering.
Check whether the unused local `sA` remained in later revisions of `ftNess_OnLoad` or was removed by cleanup.
Look for other removed inline asm blocks where nearby `#pragma peephole` directives became unnecessary.
